query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "foundation protocol: a coordination layer for agentic society" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) by investigating the same core mechanism (using conventions to coordinate agents under partial observability and limited communication) and domain (multi-agent reinforcement learning in cooperative settings) as the user's question, even though it uses the specific game of Hanabi as an empirical testbed rather than a general "agentic society" framework. It provides the canonical prior work on the specific technique of "conventions" for improving multi-age
